articles.title: Hungary Election Results: Viktor Orban Concedes Defeat and Congratulates Peter Magyar
articles.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban, who has inspired populist movements globally, could not overcome the growing dissatisfaction of his own citizens.

articles.title: Who Is Peter Magyar, the Man Who Toppled Hungary’s Orban
articles.summary: Mr. Magyar’s success in the Hungarian election is fueled in large part by widespread public anger at corruption and concern about sluggish economic growth.

articles.title: Hungary's Viktor Orbán concedes defeat, ending 16 years in power
articles.summary: Hungarian voters turned out in the greatest numbers since the 1990s to turn away from Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's right-wing populist Fidesz party, putting an end to Orbán's 16 years in power.

articles.title: Iran’s Top Negotiator Says Talks Failed Because U.S. Failed to Win Trust
articles.summary: Iranian officials said that while Iran had approached the negotiations in good faith, the U.S. team had failed to gain the Iranian delegation’s trust.

articles.title: How JD Vance Tried and Failed to End the War in Iran That He Opposed
articles.summary: Vice President JD Vance led the highest-level talks between the United States and Iran in nearly 50 years.

articles.title: California Democrats Rushed to Back Eric Swalwell — and Quickly Abandoned Him
articles.summary: Representative Eric Swalwell, a Washington figure, was largely unknown in the California State Capitol. But influential groups began backing him for governor in the weeks before he was publicly accuse

articles.title: A country-by-country glance at Pope Leo XIV's trip to Africa
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV's four-nation, 11-day trip to Africa is so dizzying in its complexity it recalls some of the globetrotting odysseys of St. John Paul II in his early years.

articles.title: What Now? Vance Leaves Iran Talks Without a Deal.
articles.summary: The lack of a breakthrough after 21 hours of negotiations leaves the Trump administration facing several unpalatable options.

articles.title: U.S. and Iran fail to reach agreement during high-level talks in Islamabad
articles.summary: The U.S and Iran did not reach an agreement to end the war in Iran at a high-level meeting on Saturday.

articles.title: A Hezbollah commander describes battling Israel in Lebanon
articles.summary: In a rare interview, a wounded Hezbollah commander tells NPR about his secretive Shia Muslim militia's new command structure and how it has managed to keep firing rockets into northern Israel.

articles.title: Trump claims U.S. will close Strait of Hormuz as Iran peace talks collapse
articles.summary: After talks between the U.S. and Iran collapsed, President Trump claimed the U.S. will "blockade" the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: The real space science behind 'Project Hail Mary'
articles.summary: The science fiction blockbuster wowed audiences with its depiction of space travel and more. Here's what NASA staff and other scientists say about the basis for the amazing events of the film.

articles.title: White House ballroom construction can continue for now, appeals court says
articles.summary: The order comes as the Trump administration challenges a lower court ruling that the estimated $300-million project requires congressional approval.

articles.title: Push to Expel Swalwell Could Touch Off Chain Reaction of House Removal Votes

articles.title: Trump touts newly released plans for D.C. triumphal arch
articles.summary: The proposed 250-feet-tall, white-and-gilded monument would stand on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., by the Potomac River.

articles.title: Pakistan hosts historic face-to-face talks between the US and Iran
articles.summary: In Pakistan's capital, Islamabad, officials from the US and Iran met to discuss a possible end to the war that's engulfed much of the Middle East.

articles.title: At a concert in Budapest, anti-Orbán sentiments take center stage ahead of election
articles.summary: At a concert in Budapest, musicians and concertgoers express criticism of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's leadership.

articles.title: How Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's hometown became a symbol of excesses
articles.summary: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán has long been accused of corruption. Sightseers now flock to his hometown as groups aim to raise awareness of what they say are the leader's excesses.

articles.title: This Hungarian Town Explains Why Orban Could Lose on Sunday
articles.summary: Lake Balaton was beloved as a vacation spot. Now, luxury real estate projects serve many friends of Prime Minister Viktor Orban, and the locals are fed up.
